Summary: Systematic: the second passage presents a role‚Üímechanism‚Üíoutcome account of PAX3-FOXO1, while the first passage reports experimental findings in ACP mice. In pediatric alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma, most tumors harbor t(2;13) or t(1;13) translocations that produce PAX3-FOXO1 or PAX7-FOXO1 fusion oncoproteins. These fusions join the PAX3/7 DNA-binding domain with the FOXO1 transactivation domain. The presence of PAX3- or PAX7-FOXO1 predicts a worse prognosis and classifying rhabdomyosarcoma by fusion-positive versus fusion-negative status better stratifies patients than histology. In ACP mice, P3F expression reprogrammed aP2-Cre‚Äìlabeled cells to a muscle stem cell phenotype and enabled muscle regeneration. Investigators followed 24 ACP mice to test whether P3F drives fusion-positive RMS. All 24 developed tumors and none developed fusion-positive RMS. The tumors included mononuclear phagocytic sarcoma and angiosarcoma. Pax3-Foxo1 expression was validated in three ACP tumors and those tumors lacked the myogenic regulatory factors Myod1 and Myog that are diagnostic for rhabdomyosarcoma. Comparison with aP2-Cre;Cdkn2a Flox mice suggests P3F expression drives diversity in the ACP tumor spectrum.
